title: CodeGen: An Open Large Language Model for Code with Multi-Turn Program Synthesis

structured.summary: The paper presents CodeGen, a large open-source language model for code, focused on improving program synthesis through multi-turn interaction. With models up to 16.1B parameters, CodeGen aims to democratize access to advanced program synthesis models and demonstrates competitive performance in zero-shot Python code generation tasks.

strengths.evidence: CodeGen offers models up to 16.1B parameters along with the open-source JAX FORMER library, facilitating research access.
The paper provides a novel multi-turn program synthesis approach, showing improved performance through factorizing specifications into subproblems.

weaknesses.point: Lack of clarity on multi-turn prompt generation
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not clearly explain the structure of multi-turn prompts in the MTPB benchmark, impacting replicability.
Evaluation primarily focuses on the HumanEval benchmark and MTPB, without extensive benchmarking against other established datasets like CodeXGLUE.

questions.question: How do multi-turn prompts impact the performance compared to single-turn prompts?
questions.motivation: Understanding the detailed impact of multi-turn prompts can clarify their advantage and improve reproducibility.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of clarity on multi-turn prompt generation. The paper does not clearly explain the structure of multi-turn prompts in the MTPB benchmark, impacting replicability
critique_points.point_002: Limited evaluation scope. Evaluation primarily focuses on the HumanEval benchmark and MTPB, without extensive benchmarking against other established datasets like CodeXGLUE
